title: Shipping & Delivery Policy
text: Last updated: 2025

title: Shipping & Delivery Policy
text:
Service Area
Pine Straw Distributors LLC primarily serves buyers in northern Florida and southern Georgia. Delivery availability outside this area is considered on a case-by-case basis. Contact us to discuss your location and volume requirements.

title: Shipping & Delivery Policy
text:
Delivery Formats
We deliver pine straw in three formats depending on order size and customer equipment:

Standard Bales — delivered by truck, no special equipment required for offloading
Palletized Bales — requires forklift or pallet jack for offloading
Round Big Bales — requires tractor equipment for handling

title: Shipping & Delivery Policy
text:
Lead Times
Delivery lead times vary by season and availability. Contact us directly for current scheduling and availability.

title: Shipping & Delivery Policy
text:
Minimum Orders
Minimum order quantities vary by delivery format. Please contact us for current minimums and pricing at jane@pinestrwadistributors.com.

title: Environmental & Sustainability Statement
text: Last updated: 2025

title: Environmental & Sustainability Statement
text:
Our Commitment to the Environment
Pine straw is a naturally renewable resource. Harvested from living pine trees without cutting them down, pine straw is raked from the forest floor as it naturally sheds — a process that can be repeated season after season without harming the trees or the surrounding ecosystem.

title: Environmental & Sustainability Statement
section: Why Pine Straw Is a Sustainable Choice
text:
Why Pine Straw Is a Sustainable Choice

No trees are cut — pine straw is a byproduct of natural needle shedding
Biodegradable — returns nutrients to the soil as it slowly breaks down
Native material — natural to the southeastern U.S. ecosystem
Reduces runoff — helps retain moisture and prevent soil erosion
Chemical-free — no pesticides or synthetic additives

title: Environmental & Sustainability Statement
text:
Our Practices
Pine Straw Distributors LLC sources from forests managed with sustainable harvesting practices. We are committed to responsible sourcing that preserves the long-term health of the pine forests we depend on.

title: Products
text: (reserved)

title: Contact
text:
Jane Doe
Jane Doe didn’t set out to build a career in pine straw — but after 25 years, she wouldn’t have it any other way. A native of the Midwest, Jane graduated from Indiana University with a degree in business and a lifelong love of the outdoors. After graduation she headed south, first settling in Georgia, where she discovered the pine straw industry almost by accident while managing a landscaping account for a golf course outside Atlanta.
That first encounter changed everything. Jane recognized immediately that pine straw was not just a mulch — it was the finishing touch that separated an average landscape from a genuinely beautiful one. She spent the next decade learning every corner of the Georgia market, building supplier relationships, and developing the logistics knowledge that would define her career.
Eventually the business — and the weather — drew her further south to Florida, where she established Pinestraw Distributors LLC and expanded her network across the full breadth of the southeastern United States. Today she sources premium pine straw from the longleaf and slash pine forests she has come to know intimately over a quarter century in the field.
Away from work, Jane is most likely to be found on a golf course — preferably one with immaculate pine straw beds lining the fairways. She believes that great landscaping and a great round of golf share the same quality: everything in its right place.

title: Contact
text:
Get in Touch
Jane Doe Principal, Pinestraw Distributors LLC

title: Employee Portal
Welcome
text: Pine straw mulch for garden

title: Welcome
text:
Who we are
Pinestraw Distributors LLC is your trusted connection to the finest pine straw mulch harvested from the longleaf and slash pine forests of northern Florida and southern Georgia. With over 25 years of experience in the market, we have built the relationships, the knowledge, and the reach to source premium-grade pine straw and deliver it to buyers who demand quality.

title: Welcome
text:
Why pinestraw?
Unlike wood chip or bark mulches that break down quickly and require frequent replacement, pine straw holds its structure season after season, maintaining a clean, finished appearance with minimal upkeep. As a product native to the southern landscape, pine straw integrates naturally into local ecosystems, supporting soil health without introducing foreign materials. It is 100% natural, chemical-free, and has the added benefit of repelling common insects — making it the smart, sustainable choice for residential landscapes, commercial properties, and large-scale agricultural applications. When you choose Pinestraw Distributors LLC, you’re choosing a mulch that works as hard as the land it comes from.

title: Welcome
text:
How We Deliver
Pine straw is available in three formats to match any job size, from a single residential yard to a large-scale commercial project. We work with you to determine the right format for your volume, equipment, and timeline.

Standard Bales
Individual hand-tied bales are the most common format for residential landscapers and smaller commercial accounts. Lightweight and easy to handle, standard bales can be distributed by hand without any special equipment. They are ideal for detailed landscape beds, tight spaces, and customers who prefer to apply mulch themselves or in small crews.


Palletized Bales
For larger orders, bales are stacked and strapped on pallets for efficient transport and handling. Palletized delivery is the preferred format for nurseries, landscape supply companies, and commercial contractors who move volume regularly. Pallets can be offloaded with a forklift or pallet jack, reducing labor and turnaround time on large jobs.


Round Big Bales
Round big bales offer maximum volume for large-scale applications such as highway landscaping, golf courses, farms, and municipal projects. These bulk bales are loaded and unloaded with tractor equipment and represent the most cost-effective per-unit price for buyers with the capacity to handle them. Contact us to discuss availability and minimum order quantities.
